Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite auth login — replace migration system with email lookup

The old AuthContext had ~130 lines of migration logic that would
delete and recreate user docs on first login, causing race conditions
that locked out 5 of 10 users (Abe, Gus, John, Leah, Phil).

New approach: query users collection by email, stamp UID on the
existing doc. No doc deletion, no migration locks, no redirect
guards. Works with both email-keyed and UID-keyed document IDs.

commit.message:
research: prove Dirichlet kernel lemmas (fourier-series-oq-03) (#3375)

* research: constructive 2D Borsuk-Ulam via Tucker lemma (borsuk-ulam-oq-03-oq-03)

Build bridge from Tucker's lemma to 2D Borsuk-Ulam theorem using
dominant component labeling. Define antisymmetric difference g(x) =
f(x) - f(-x), prove oddness and continuity, formalize triangulated
disk structure and grid triangulation. State approximate and exact
BU theorems. 7 proved, 5 sorries, 2 axioms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: prove Dirichlet kernel lemmas and continuity consequence (fourier-series-oq-03)

Resolve 3 of 4 sorries in FourierSeriesOQ03.lean:
- dirichletKernel_at_zero: D_N(0) = 2N+1 via fourier_at_zero helper
- dirichletKernel_neg: conjugate symmetry via fourier_apply_neg/fourier_eval_neg
- dirichlet_at_continuity_point: Fourier converges to f(x₀) at continuity points

New helper lemmas: fourier_at_zero, fourier_apply_neg, fourier_eval_neg,
rightLimit_eq_of_continuousAt, leftLimit_eq_of_continuousAt

Remaining sorry: dirichlet_pointwise_convergence (main theorem, needs ~300 lines
of Riemann-Lebesgue + integral splitting infrastructure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
research: formalize smooth Gauss-Bonnet theorem (euler-polyhedral-formula-oq-02-oq-01) (#3376)

Formalizes ∫_M K dA = 2πχ(M) for compact Riemannian surfaces:
- CompactRiemannianSurface structure with Gauss-Bonnet axiom
- Genus determination: K>0→sphere, K=0→torus, K<0→genus≥2
- Total curvature as topological invariant (metric-independent)
- Average curvature formulas and area bounds
- Connection to discrete Gauss-Bonnet (same 2πχ)
- Chern-Gauss-Bonnet generalization (axiomatic)
- Concrete examples: sphere (K=1), torus (K=0), genus 2 (K=-1)

Core theorem axiomatized (Mathlib lacks Riemannian geometry).
20+ consequence theorems, 0 sorries, Docker-verified.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: lean-genius
repository.description: Annotations for Lean Proofs

commit.message:
fix: tighten TOD pps_delay threshold from 990 ms to 200 ms

A scheduling delay in the Python reader thread can cause the TOD packet
to be stamped up to ~500 ms late.  Values above ~200 ms produce an NTP
offset for SHM(1) that disagrees with the corrected SHM(0) offset
(~-20 ms), causing ntpd's intersection algorithm to flag both sources as
falsetickers when there are only two sources available.

Normal TOD packet delivery takes 10–50 ms after the PPS edge; 200 ms
gives 4× headroom for moderate system load while still rejecting the
pathological late-delivery cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct SHM0 clock timestamp for next-second TIME:STRing? response

TIME:STRing? returns the GPS time for the *next* PPS tick (standard GPS
receiver behaviour).  Using gps_time directly as clockTimeStamp caused
SHM(0) to show ~+978 ms offset (≈ 1 s − SCPI RTT), which placed it near
the NTP falseticker threshold and destabilised clock selection when SHM(1)
was temporarily unavailable.

Subtract 1 s to obtain the actual GPS second at recv_time; SHM(0) offset
is now ≈ −(SCPI RTT) ≈ −20 ms, in agreement with SHM(1) PPS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
CRITICAL SECURITY FIX: Remove exposed Supabase API keys

- Remove exposed SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from client-side code
- Implement secure server-side API endpoint for booking submissions
- Add environment variable configuration with .env.example
- Configure security headers in vercel.json (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options)
- Update .gitignore to protect sensitive environment files
- Create deployment checklist with critical action items

HIPAA COMPLIANCE: This fix addresses a critical security vulnerability
where Protected Health Information (PHI) database credentials were
exposed in client-side JavaScript. All API calls now route through
secure server-side endpoints with proper access controls.

Related: HIPAA Security Rule compliance, BAA requirements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(eczema): fix zone selection accuracy + drawing at all zoom levels

Root causes fixed:
1. Zone selection hitting wrong zones (e.g. tap z17 selects z19):
   - Expanded parent gave tight height constraints exceeding w*aspect,
     stretching the widget beyond _sz.height. _toImage() Y-scaling was wrong.
   - Fix: LayoutBuilder now computes fitted size respecting aspect ratio
     within available space, wrapped in Center widget.

2. Freehand drawing invisible/inconsistent (worked 1 in 8 tries):
   - GestureDetector.onPan inside InteractiveViewer lost gesture arena
     even with panEnabled:false because scaleEnabled:true still competed.
   - Fix: Both panEnabled and scaleEnabled set to false in drawMode.

3. Zone selection wrong when zoomed (new bug caught by research):
   - Inner GestureDetector localPosition is in zoomed space (0..2*w at 2x)
     but _toImage divided by unzoomed _sz, giving wrong coordinates.
   - Fix: Moved GestureDetector OUTSIDE InteractiveViewer. Uses
     TransformationController.toScene() to convert viewport coords to
     content-space coords at any zoom/pan level.

File: lib/widgets/eczema_body_map.dart
- _viewportToImage() replaces _contentToImage/_toImage
- GestureDetector wraps InteractiveViewer (not inside it)
- Listener for pointer tracking wraps both
- Aspect-ratio-safe layout with Center + fitted SizedBox

🎨 Align TypeScript syntax highlighting with JavaScript

Add explicit highlight groups for yats (vim-polyglot) TypeScript syntax
so TS files use the same color scheme as JS files:
- Function args/params → blue (was falling through to purple)
- Function names/member calls → yellow (matching jsFuncCall)
- Class/interface/async keywords → green (matching jsFunction/jsStorageClass)
- this/super/globals → purple (matching jsThis/jsGlobalObjects)
- Template literals → cyan (matching jsTemplateString)
- Type annotations/references → blue (matching identifiers)
- Try/catch/cast keywords → orange (matching special)
- Commas/accessors/optional marks → grey (matching noise)

commit.message:
research: formalize Cauchy-Schwarz equality characterization (cauchy-schwarz-oq-03-oq-01) (#3374)

Proves when equality holds in Cauchy-Schwarz via the Lagrange identity:
- Lagrange identity: ∑ᵢ∑ⱼ(fᵢgⱼ-fⱼgᵢ)² = 2[(∑f²)(∑g²)-(∑fg)²]
- Full iff: CS equality ⟺ all cross-terms vanish (proportionality)
- Inner product space: ‖⟨u,v⟩‖ = ‖u‖·‖v‖ ⟺ ∃c, u = c•v
- Independent CS proof from Lagrange identity
- Hölder inequality for NNReal finite sums

10+ theorems, 0 sorries, Docker-verified.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: raise TOD recv delay threshold to 990ms, rate-limit warning

After a restart with an unsynchronised system clock the packet arrival
delay can be ~800 ms permanently, blocking all SHM1 writes and
preventing NTP from ever correcting the clock (chicken-and-egg).

Raise the threshold from 700 ms to 990 ms so NTP sees the offset and
can step/slew the clock.  The BCD-second check already rejects packets
with delays > ~1 s, so 990 ms is the safe upper bound.

Also rate-limit the delay warning to once per 30 s to avoid log spam.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: upgrade auditor to Scout — autonomous 3-layer codebase analyst (#37)

Transforms the basic auditor (scanner + task_writer) into Scout, a
multi-layer autonomous analysis engine that produces prioritized,
batch-ready task queues for overnight execution.

Layer 1 (static): error handling gaps, dead code, code smells, test
coverage gaps, deep nesting, magic numbers, duplicate strings — on top
of the original missing docs, TODOs, and complexity checks.

Layer 2 (deps): OSV.dev integration scans lockfiles (requirements.txt,
Cargo.lock, package-lock.json, go.sum, etc.) for known CVEs with zero
config and no API key.

Layer 3 (scout brain): LLM-powered creative analysis identifies feature
opportunities, implicit bug risks, performance issues, and architectural
improvements that static analysis cannot find.

Task writer now produces priority-ordered tasks (security > bugs > tests
> refactor > cleanup > docs > features) with effort estimates and a
queue manifest for team review before batch runs.

https://claude.ai/code/session_011K4y2nSumuVHtvrdfnqpdg

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
